organization.name: Rootz Corp
organization.mission: Every AI output should have a provable origin — who authorized the agent, what model answered, when it happened.

site.name: Proof of Origin
site.description: Documentation and live demonstration of AI agents with birth certificates, cryptographic audit chains, and signed session archives on the Polygon blockchain. Built on Morpheus decentralized inference.

technology.description: Morpheus AI agent as a Rootz data wallet — every inference call signed, hash-linked, and settled on-chain

proof.description: Live on-chain proof artifacts from the first AI agent with a birth certificate

fourLayers.description: Four layers of provable AI, each adding trust

layers.name: Agent-Side Signing
layers.description: Agent signs prompt hashes, hashes responses, chain-links every action, settles Merkle root on-chain

layers.name: Provider-Side Signing
layers.description: Provider signs responses with registered wallet — bilateral proof of conversation

layers.name: Encrypted Channel
layers.description: ECDH key exchange, AES-256-GCM encryption, TEE attestation proves the enclave

layers.name: Model Provenance
layers.description: Model built from signed sources — training data manifests, weight hashes, build attestation

repositories.name: morpheus-agent
repositories.description: Morpheus AI agent as a Rootz data wallet

concepts.definition: A permanent on-chain record created when an AI agent is born, naming its two parents: the AI model that powers it and the human who authorized it.
Cryptographic evidence linking an AI output to a specific agent, model, and authorizer — signed, hash-linked, and blockchain-anchored.
An encrypted on-chain data wallet that serves as an AI agent's permanent identity and audit trail. Notes accumulate as the agent's chain of experience.
A Merkle root covering all actions in a session, written as a single on-chain Note. One transaction proves the integrity of the entire session.
A signed communication layer between agent and inference provider. Layer 1: agent signs. Layer 2: provider signs. Layer 3: ECDH encrypted.
Not storage — a messaging framework. Write to an address, all devices watching receive the update. Carries data, code, policy, instructions, and receipts.
